title: Bech32m Addresses
description: Bech32m is the checksum format used for native segregated witness addresses with version 1 and higher. It preserves the human-readable, error-detecting structure of Bech32 while changing the checksum constant so wallets can distinguish newer witness versions such as Taproot from version-0 segwit addresses.

resources.title: BIP 350: Bech32m Format for v1+ Witness Addresses
resources.notes: Canonical definition of Bech32m and its requirement for native witness version 1 and higher outputs.

resources.title: Bitcoin Optech Topic: Bech32(m)
resources.notes: Practical overview of Bech32 and Bech32m as native segwit address formats and how witness versioning affects encoding.

resources.title: Mastering Bitcoin
resources.notes: Address-format chapter covering Bech32/Bech32m structure, witness versions, and modern wallet address handling.
